title: Interfaces, Dynamic Types, Errors, and API Boundaries · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A notification service must accept narrow capabilities, preserve error causes, and distinguish nil interfaces from typed nil values. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a capability-oriented notification boundary after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define small consumer-owned interfaces and use implicit satisfaction without premature abstraction.
Trace interface dynamic type and value, nil interfaces, typed nil pointers, comparison, and method dispatch.
Use checked type assertions and type switches only where dynamic behavior is part of the boundary contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
